title: Multiversion Concurrency Control (MVCC) Snapshot Isolation
description: A diagram illustrating how MVCC maintains multiple versions of a data item to provide each transaction with a consistent snapshot of the database, enabling read operations to proceed without blocking write operations.
elements: Database with Versioned Data Items (e.g., X_v1, X_v2, Y_v1)
relationships: T1 reads from the snapshot of committed versions that existed at its start time
T2 creates a new version (e.g., X_v2) while T1 continues to read the old version (X_v1)
T2 commits, making its new version the latest committed version
A subsequent transaction T3 would see T2's committed version (X_v2) in its snapshot
